page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is poor, falling into the bottom 10%, while multi-core CPU performance is average. However, the server demonstrates strong multi-threaded capabilities in specific workloads, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, PassMark CPU score, extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests all scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth and database operations consistently perform in the average tier.

Due to the shared CPU allocation, single-threaded execution speeds are limited, making this server less suitable for latency-sensitive single-core tasks. However, the high memory capacity and strong multi-threaded performance make it highly resource-dense and cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits poor single-core performance but delivers strong multi-threaded capabilities, with PassMark CPU and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier. Given the shared CPU allocation and high memory density, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy, parallelized workloads. It is best suited for large-scale database deployments, multi-threaded data processing, and software compilation tasks that benefit from substantial memory capacity.

tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring forty-eight shared cores and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for parallelized, memory-heavy workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
